what_changed: Local recall-loss reports now include import_snapshot_census.
The census records successful ImportedLiteralSnapshot evidence counts by language.
The census records unresolved binding-import miss reasons with stable module/export hashes, importer locations, and provider locations when a provider candidate exists.
Provider RHS rejection reasons are read from a reporting-only helper next to imported_literal_export_safe, so diagnostics reuse the same provider export contract without admitting new exact paths.
The report remains local-only and does not include raw source snippets.

notes: This phase is reporting-only; it does not open new imported snapshots or change clone-family admission.
The full crates report now exposes import snapshot misses even when oracle completeness is unchanged.
The +5 admission rejections come from additional Rust implementation/test units introduced by this reporting change; hard gates and under-merge counts do not move.

The census shows that the crates workspace has no successful imported literal snapshots today, and that most unresolved binding imports are module/export resolution scope rather than provider factory semantics.
decision.follow_up: Use import_snapshot_census to choose the next imported-value slice. On crates, provider-module-missing and provider-export-missing dominate; the small provider-aggregate-children-not-exact-safe group is the immediate semantic export-safety candidate.
